components.label: TEST PROGRESS
components.note: Clicking a blue/brown link navigates to that question

components.label: Suspend Session
components.behaviour: On resume, test starts from the suspended question

partial.description: Each correct item scores 1; incorrect scores 0

negative.description: Correct=+1, Incorrect=-1; floor at 0 if negatives exceed positives
negative.floor_rule: If incorrect_count > correct_count → score = 0, not negative

rank.description: Highest rank receives n (total options), lowest receives 1

rank_x_rate.example: Rank 1 of 4 options (score=4) × Rating 6 of 7 = 24; Max = 4×7 = 28

rating_scale.description: Point on scale maps to score; forward or reverse direction configurable

forced_choice.description: Score assigned per option at question creation; may be 0&1, 1&2, 1&3 etc.

question_types.label: Multiple Choice – Text/Image
behaviour: Single correct answer only
Submit saves answer and loads next question
Bookmark flags question and navigates to next

question_types.label: Multiple Choice – Text/Image with Image Response Options

content_fields.note: Image options take significantly more vertical space than text options

behaviour: Single correct answer only
Option images may be large; layout must account for extra vertical space

question_types.label: Multiple Choice – Audio with Multiple Questions

components.note: Play once only; no replay after initial play

prev_btn.label: Previous Question
prev_btn.note: Returns to previous sub-question of this audio; no audio replay

behaviour: Audio plays once on display screen; NO REPLAY ever
Navigating back to this question only shows answer options, never re-plays audio
Each sub-question gets individual score of 0 or 1
Number of sub-questions defined at question creation

question_types.label: Multiple Choice – Video with Multiple Questions
behaviour: Video plays once; NO REPLAY ever
Navigating back only shows answer options, never re-plays video
Each sub-question scores 0 or 1 independently

question_types.label: Multiple Choice – Word Flash with Multiple Questions
behaviour: Words randomly selected from a larger list
Display count (how many words flash) set at creation
Time interval per word set at creation
NO REPLAY of flash list ever
Navigating back only shows answer options

question_types.label: Multiple Choice – Image Flash with Multiple Questions
behaviour: Images randomly selected from larger list
NO REPLAY of image flash list ever

question_types.label: Multiple Choice – Passage Display with Multiple Questions
behaviour: Passage shown for a defined timed interval then auto-disappears
NO REPEAT DISPLAY of passage once timer expires
Navigating back only shows answer options, never re-shows passage

question_types.label: Multiple Choice – Image Display with Multiple Questions
behaviour: Image shown for defined timed interval then auto-disappears
NO REPEAT DISPLAY of image once timer expires
Navigating back only shows answer options, never re-shows image

question_types.label: Fill-in-the-Blank – Single Field

percentage_match.note: If % match set at 100, all letters must match; letter-by-letter comparison

behaviour: Up to 5 predefined correct answers; any match → score 1
Case sensitivity configurable at creation via checkbox
Percentage match mode: single correct answer, letter-by-letter comparison

question_types.label: Fill-in-the-Blank – Multiple Fields
behaviour: Each field scored independently: correct=1, incorrect=0
Total score = sum of correct field scores
Max score = number of answer fields
Up to 5 possible correct answers per field
Number of fields defined at creation

question_types.label: Fill-in-the-Blank – Word Flash with Multiple Recall Fields

components.note: Field count adjustable at creation; candidate types what they remember

behaviour: Candidate recalls and types words they remember from the flash list
Each correctly recalled word scores 1
7 correct recalls → total score 7
Fields can be added/removed at creation time
NO REPLAY of flash list

question_types.label: Fill-in-the-Blank – Image Flash with Multiple Recall Fields
behaviour: Candidate recalls and types names of images they remember
Each correctly recalled image name scores 1
NO REPLAY of image flash list

question_types.label: Match-the-Following – Drag and Drop

scoring.example: 5 matches all correct → score 5; 3 correct → score 3

drag_pool.label: DRAG & DROP
drag_pool.note: Pool has more options than match slots to prevent guessing

behaviour: Candidate drags from DRAG & DROP pool into GROUP B slots
Pool always has more items than match slots
Each correctly placed item scores 1
Max score = number of match pairs

question_types.label: Grid-List Selection – Multiple Answers

content_fields.label: View Complete Grid Items
content_fields.note: Full list uses same numbering as grid

behaviour: Candidate clicks cells to open popup showing full option text
SELECT in popup confirms that option
Candidate can revisit and modify selections
Negative marking: incorrect -1, but floor at 0

question_types.label: HOTSPOT Selection – Single Answer per Question

scoring.note: Click on hotspot=1, non-hotspot=0, no negative

behaviour: Only the LATEST click is scored; previous clicks ignored
Click on non-hotspot pixel is invalid → score 0, no negative
Multiple sub-questions can be based on one pixelised figure
Each sub-question appears one at a time
Hotspot area pixel size defined at question creation

question_types.label: HOTSPOT Selection – Multiple Answers

per_click_flow.step1: click → red button marker appears at click location
per_click_flow.step3b: CANCEL → click invalidated and not counted

behaviour: All confirmed clicks are considered for scoring
Each click marked with red button indicator until confirmed/cancelled
Negative marking for wrong clicks; floor at 0

question_types.label: Simple Ranking Scale – Psychometric

scoring.description: Rank 1 (highest) = n points; last rank = 1 point

components.options: Rank 1 to Rank n (defined at creation)

behaviour: Option count = number of sections in the assessment
Each option is tagged to exactly one section
Candidate clicks RANK button → popup → selects rank
Rank 1 = highest priority/preference

question_types.label: First Rank-Then Rate Scale – Psychometric

rating_scale.label: Rate the selected option on the SCALE below:

two_level_flow.level_1: Candidate clicks RANK button → selects rank from popup
two_level_flow.level_2: Rating scale appears → candidate selects rating point

behaviour: Two-level interaction per option: rank first, then rate
Rating scale extent defined at creation
Option count = number of sections in assessment
Each option tagged to exactly one section

question_types.label: Standard Rating Scale – Psychometric

content_fields.example: Indicate how true the following statements are...

behaviour: All questions displayed in continuous scroll
Display order can be random or static
Each rating click highlights selected cell
Forward or reverse scoring direction set at creation

question_types.label: Forced-Choice Scale – Single Level Rating

scoring.note: Score values assigned to each option at creation; may be 0&1, 1&2, 1&3 etc.

content_fields.example: Choose the statement that most closely describes your Self. BE SPONTANEOUS.

behaviour: Each question has exactly two options (both are questions, not simple answers)
Candidate selects one option by clicking its blue button
Options are tagged to different sections of the assessment
Score values are pre-defined at creation; not always 0&1
All questions displayed in continuous scroll

question_types.label: Forced-Choice Scale – Two-Level Rating

content_fields.example: Choose the statement that most closely describes your Self. BE SPONTANEOUS.

content_fields.label: Rate the selected option on the SCALE below:

two_level_flow.level_1: Candidate clicks blue button beside chosen option
two_level_flow.level_2: Rating scale appears below → candidate selects rating

behaviour: Two-level: select option first, then rate intensity on scale
Rating scale defined at creation (e.g. 1–7 points)
Pre-defined option scores set at creation; not always 0&1
Each question has exactly two options tagged to different sections

creation_time_config_summary.description: Parameters that must be configured at question creation time for each type

component_library.description: Reusable component definitions referenced across question types
